    About Me

    I’m a PhD Candidate in Robotics. My work focuses on enhancing the robustness of navigation in challenging environments, considering perception quality, specialized for underwater robots.

    Current Research Topics

    • Underwater Robotics
    • Active SLAM & Active Localization
    • Perception-Aware Control
    • Robust Control

    Education

    Joint PhD Candidate
    August 2024 — Present

    • Home Institute: The University of Melbourne, Australia
      • Supervisors: Dr. Ye Pu, Prof. Ying Tan, Prof. Jason Monty
    • Host Institute: Shanghai Jiao Tong University, China
      • Supervisor: Prof. Weidong Zhang
    • Thesis title: Vision-Based Perception-Aware Navigation for Underwater Robots

    Bachelor of Mechatronics Engineering
    September 2019 — September 2023

    • Institution: Hanoi University of Science and Technology, Viet Nam
    • Graduation Thesis: Hedge-algebra-based formation controller for non-holonomic mobile robots with overarching supervision.

    Teaching Experience

    The University of Melbourne | Subject Demonstrator
    Reinforcement Learning for Engineering

    • Developed workshop instructions and finished source code for the subject’s first-ever delivery.
    • Demonstrated weekly workshop sessions.
    • Assessed workshop and final reports to assist with final grading.

    VinUniversity | Full-Time Teaching Assistant

    • Supported the academic delivery and technical facilitation of eight engineering courses, with a strong focus on mechatronics and control systems.
